Maternal & Child Health
Maternal & Child Health
Our Community Health Division administers various programs related to Maternal and Child Health. The programs consist of the following:
Butte / Glenn County Child Death Review 2010 to 2015 Report
California Children's Services (CCS)
CCS provides case management and physical and occupational therapy to children 0 to 21 years of age with CCS medically eligible conditions.
Visit the
California Children's Services (CCS) page
for more information.
Childhood Lead Poisoning Prevention Program (CLPPP)
The Butte County Childhood Lead Poisoning Prevention Program (CLPPP) conducts outreach and education about lead poisoning and provides nurse case management in the event of a lead-poisoned child.
Visit the
Childhood Lead Poisoning Prevention Program (CLPPP) page
for more information.
Enhanced Care Management (ECM)
ECM provides care management to eligible Partnership HealthPlan members.
Visit the
Enhanced Care Management page
for more information.
Maternal Child Adolescent Health Program (MCAH)
The Maternal Child Adolescent Health Program (MCAH) goal is to help protect and improve the health of Butte County's reproductive-age women, infants, children, adolescents, and their families.
Visit the
Maternal Child Adolescent Health Program (MCAH) page
for more information.
Nurse-Family Partnership Program (NFP)
Nurse-Family Partnership is a free home visiting program that partners registered nurses with first-time moms beginning early in their pregnancy (before 28 weeks). Moms have the opportunity to continue receiving support from the registered nurse until their child is 2 years of age. Specially trained nurses provide education, case management, and support of client goals. Nurses offer guidance on how to have a healthy pregnancy, prepare for the birth of the child, and understand child health and development outcomes
.
View the
Nurse-Family Partnership Program page
for more information.
SIDS / SUID Support & Info
The SIDS/SUID Support information will help you keep your baby safe from suffocation, Sudden Infant Death Syndrome (SIDS), and accidents during sleep.
Visit the
SIDS / SUID Support and Info page
for more information.
For more information on these programs, you may visit the specific webpage for that program.
